Name: Requiring the department of sanitation to clean and maintain all city-owned vacant lots.
Title: A Local Law to amend the administrative code of the city of New York, in relation to requiring the department of sanitation to clean and maintain all city-owned vacant lots
BodyName: Committee on Sanitation and Solid Waste Management

History.Description: This Introduction was Introduced by Council
This Introduction was Referred to Comm by Council to the Committee on Sanitation and Solid Waste Management

Summary: This bill would require that the Department of Sanitation be the sole agency responsible for cleaning and maintaining all City-owned vacant lots and the sidewalks abutting such lots, regardless of which City agency owns such vacant lot.
Text:
Be it enacted by the Council as follows:
      
      
      Section 1. Subparagraph (b) of paragraph 2 of section 16-118 of the administrative code of the city of New York is amended by adding a new clause (1) to read as follows:
      (1) For vacant lots owned by the city and for which the city regularly pays all or part of the annual bills, the department shall perform the requirements of subparagraph (b) of paragraph 2 of this section.
      � 2. This local law takes effect immediately.

MB
LS # 21458
03/23/2026
